page.title: Dietary Restrictions on Yacht Catering in Marbella: What You Need to Know

data.meta_description: Learn how to handle dietary restrictions on yacht catering in Marbella. Our fleet offers custom menus for allergies, gluten-free, vegan & halal. Book now.
data.summary: A practical guide to managing dietary restrictions on yacht catering in Marbella, covering allergies, gluten-free, vegan, halal, and kosher needs with tips on communicating with skippers and provisioning.
key_facts: All charters include water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks as standard.
Custom catering for dietary restrictions (vegan, gluten-free, halal, allergies) is available on request.
Notify us at least 48 hours before departure to guarantee dietary accommodation.
Our fleet departs exclusively from Puerto Banús; provisioning is sourced from Marbella and Estepona.
No extra charge for standard dietary modifications; premium bespoke menus may incur a supplement.
All food and drink is prepared onshore and brought aboard; no cooking facilities on most boats.
Sample menus for gluten-free, vegan, and halal are available via WhatsApp before booking.

faq.q: Can you accommodate gluten-free or coeliac-safe meals on a Marbella yacht charter?
faq.a: Yes, we can arrange gluten-free catering for all our charters. Our standard light snacks include gluten-free options, and we can provision a full gluten-free menu (e.g., salads, grilled fish, rice dishes) from trusted suppliers in Marbella and Estepona. Please inform us at least 48 hours in advance so we can avoid cross-contamination.

faq.q: Is halal food available on yacht charters in Marbella?
faq.a: Halal catering can be arranged on request. We work with halal-certified caterers in Puerto Banús and Marbella to provide meat, poultry, and snacks that meet halal standards. All alcohol served is separate and clearly labelled. Let us know your requirements at booking so we can coordinate the menu.

faq.q: Can you provide vegan or plant-based meals on a boat trip?
faq.a: Absolutely. Vegan options are popular and easy to arrange. Our standard snack platter can be replaced with hummus, vegetable crudités, fresh fruit, and plant-based wraps. For longer charters (4+ hours), we can prepare a full vegan menu including mains like grilled vegetables, quinoa salads, and dairy-free desserts.

faq.q: What about kosher food on a yacht charter in Marbella?
faq.a: Kosher catering is possible but requires more lead time due to sourcing. We can arrange kosher-certified meals from suppliers in Marbella or Málaga, including pre-packaged kosher meals and fresh produce. Please contact us at least one week in advance to discuss your specific kashrut level and budget.

faq.q: Do you cater for nut allergies or other serious food allergies?
faq.a: Yes, we take allergies seriously. Our standard snacks are nut-free upon request, and we can avoid all common allergens (dairy, eggs, shellfish, soy) with advance notice. We recommend bringing your own EpiPen if you have severe allergies, as our crew are trained in basic first aid but not medical professionals.

faq.q: Is there an extra charge for dietary-restricted menus on your boats?
faq.a: For most dietary modifications (e.g., gluten-free snacks, vegan mains, halal meat), there is no extra charge if requested at least 48 hours before departure. Premium bespoke menus (e.g., kosher certified, multiple-course gourmet) may incur a small supplement, typically €15–€30 per person. We will confirm any extra costs before you book.

faq.q: Can I bring my own food if I have specific dietary needs?
faq.a: Yes, you are welcome to bring your own food and drinks aboard any of our charters. Our crew can store and serve your provisions. However, our standard included catering already covers most dietary requirements, so check with us first—you may not need to bring anything.

faq.q: How do I communicate my dietary restrictions for a yacht charter in Marbella?
faq.a: Simply mention your dietary needs when you enquire via WhatsApp or email. We will send you a menu options sheet and confirm the final menu 48 hours before sailing. For complex restrictions (e.g., multiple allergies, kosher), we recommend a phone call to ensure nothing is missed.

data.body_html:
When chartering a yacht in Marbella, the standard catering—water, soft drinks, beer, cava, white wine, and light snacks—covers most guests. But if you or your party have specific dietary restrictions, you need to know what's possible, what's not, and how to plan ahead. This guide covers everything from gluten-free and vegan to halal and kosher catering on our fleet departing from Puerto Banús.

Our Standard Inclusions and How They Adapt

Every charter from our fleet includes a licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, Spanish IVA, and a catering package: bottled water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks (typically olives, crisps, nuts, and finger sandwiches). These can be adjusted for dietary needs at no extra cost when requested 48 hours in advance. For example, we swap standard crisps for gluten-free crackers, or replace sandwiches with vegetable platters for vegan guests.

Gluten-Free and Coeliac-Safe Options

Gluten-free catering is straightforward in Marbella. Our suppliers in Estepona and Marbella provide gluten-free bread, pasta, and snacks. For coeliac guests, we ensure no cross-contamination by using separate preparation surfaces and sealed packaging. Sample gluten-free menu: grilled chicken skewers, quinoa salad, fresh fruit, and gluten-free brownies. Let us know at booking so we can confirm the menu.

Vegan and Plant-Based Catering

Vegan options are popular and easy to arrange. Our standard snack platter can be replaced with hummus, baba ganoush, vegetable crudités, olives, and fresh fruit. For longer charters (4+ hours, such as on the Mangusta 80 or Ferretti 94), we offer mains like vegetable paella, lentil salads, and dairy-free desserts. All drinks included are vegan-friendly (check cava—most is, but we confirm).

Halal Catering on Board

Halal meat and poultry are available from certified suppliers in Marbella and Málaga. We can arrange halal chicken skewers, lamb kofta, and mixed grills. All alcohol is served separately and clearly labelled. Non-alcoholic beverages are included as standard. Please specify halal requirements at the time of booking so we can coordinate with our caterers.

Kosher and Other Religious Dietary Needs

Kosher catering requires more lead time due to sourcing. We can arrange pre-packaged kosher meals from Málaga or Marbella, or work with a kosher caterer for fresh options. Expect a lead time of at least one week. Costs may be slightly higher (€15–€30 supplement per person). We also accommodate Hindu (vegetarian) and Jain (no onion/garlic) diets on request.

Allergies and Intolerances

We handle nut, dairy, egg, shellfish, and soy allergies. Our standard snacks are nut-free upon request. For severe allergies, we recommend bringing your own EpiPen—our crew are first-aid trained but not medical professionals. We label all food clearly and avoid shared utensils. Just tell us at booking, and we'll send a detailed ingredient list before departure.

How to Book with Dietary Restrictions

To ensure your dietary needs are met, contact us via WhatsApp or email at least 48 hours before your charter. We'll send a menu options sheet and confirm everything in writing. For complex needs (multiple allergies, kosher), a phone call is best. All our boats—from the compact Speedboat to the spacious Ferretti 94—depart from Puerto Banús, and provisioning is handled locally.

Frequently Asked Questions

Can you accommodate gluten-free or coeliac-safe meals on a Marbella yacht charter?

Yes, we can arrange gluten-free catering for all our charters. Our standard light snacks include gluten-free options, and we can provision a full gluten-free menu (e.g., salads, grilled fish, rice dishes) from trusted suppliers in Marbella and Estepona. Please inform us at least 48 hours in advance so we can avoid cross-contamination.

Is halal food available on yacht charters in Marbella?

Halal catering can be arranged on request. We work with halal-certified caterers in Puerto Banús and Marbella to provide meat, poultry, and snacks that meet halal standards. All alcohol served is separate and clearly labelled. Let us know your requirements at booking so we can coordinate the menu.

Can you provide vegan or plant-based meals on a boat trip?

Absolutely. Vegan options are popular and easy to arrange. Our standard snack platter can be replaced with hummus, vegetable crudités, fresh fruit, and plant-based wraps. For longer charters (4+ hours), we can prepare a full vegan menu including mains like grilled vegetables, quinoa salads, and dairy-free desserts.

What about kosher food on a yacht charter in Marbella?

Kosher catering is possible but requires more lead time due to sourcing. We can arrange kosher-certified meals from suppliers in Marbella or Málaga, including pre-packaged kosher meals and fresh produce. Please contact us at least one week in advance to discuss your specific kashrut level and budget.

Do you cater for nut allergies or other serious food allergies?

Yes, we take allergies seriously. Our standard snacks are nut-free upon request, and we can avoid all common allergens (dairy, eggs, shellfish, soy) with advance notice. We recommend bringing your own EpiPen if you have severe allergies, as our crew are trained in basic first aid but not medical professionals.

Is there an extra charge for dietary-restricted menus on your boats?

For most dietary modifications (e.g., gluten-free snacks, vegan mains, halal meat), there is no extra charge if requested at least 48 hours before departure. Premium bespoke menus (e.g., kosher certified, multiple-course gourmet) may incur a small supplement, typically €15–€30 per person. We will confirm any extra costs before you book.

Can I bring my own food if I have specific dietary needs?

Yes, you are welcome to bring your own food and drinks aboard any of our charters. Our crew can store and serve your provisions. However, our standard included catering already covers most dietary requirements, so check with us first—you may not need to bring anything.

How do I communicate my dietary restrictions for a yacht charter in Marbella?

Simply mention your dietary needs when you enquire via WhatsApp or email. We will send you a menu options sheet and confirm the final menu 48 hours before sailing. For complex restrictions (e.g., multiple allergies, kosher), we recommend a phone call to ensure nothing is missed.

Planning a charter with specific dietary needs? Browse our full fleet and let us know your requirements—we'll handle the rest.
